LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA TOW TRUCK'S ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S FACIAL CHALLENGE IS DENIED. U.S. V. CLINICAL LEASING SERVICE, INC. (5TH CIR. 1991) 925 F.2D 120, 123 IS PERSUASIVE AUTHORITY SUPPORTING DEFENDANT'S POSITION. DEFENDANT CONTENDS THAT THE TOWING PERMIT SCHEME IS ONLY BEING APPLIED TO THOSE DRIVERS AND FIRMS THAT CONDUCT SUBSTANTIAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL BUSINESS IN SAN FRANCISCO. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE TOWING PERMIT SCHEME IS BEING APPLIED TO DRIVERS AND FIRMS THAT PERFORM MINIMAL OR TRANSITORY WORK IN THE CITY. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE PERMIT FEES ARE IMPERMISSIBLE TAXES, I.E. THE FEES ARE APPLIED FOR GENERAL REVENUE PURPOSES OR ARE UNREASONABLY EXCESSIVE WHEN COMPARED TO THE REGULATING ACTIVITY. 1 ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S CROSS-MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FACIAL CHALLENGE IS DENIED. U.S. V. CLINICAL LEASING SERVICE, INC. (5TH CIR. 1991) 925 F.2D 120, 123 IS PERSUASIVE AUTHORITY SUPPORTING DEFENDANT'S POSITION. DEFENDANT CONTENDS THAT THE TOWING PERMIT SCHEME IS ONLY BEING APPLIED TO THOSE DRIVERS AND FIRMS THAT CONDUCT SUBSTANTIAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL BUSINESS IN SAN FRANCISCO. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE TOWING PERMIT SCHEME IS BEING APPLIED TO DRIVERS AND FIRMS THAT PERFORM MINIMAL OR TRANSITORY WORK IN THE CITY. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE PERMIT FEES ARE IMPERMISSIBLE TAXES, I.E. THE FEES ARE APPLIED FOR GENERAL REVENUE PURPOSES OR ARE UNREASONABLY EXCESSIVE WHEN COMPARED TO THE REGULATING ACTIVITY. 1 ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. PATRICK WHALEN & ATTY. BROOKS ELLISON OF ELLISON WILSON ADVOCACY LL, TEL. NO. (916) 448-2187 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. VINCE CHHABRIA, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4674 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Cross-Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff's facial challenge is denied. U.S. v. Clinical Leasing Service, Inc. (5th Cir. 1991) 925 F.2d 120, 123 is persuasive authority supporting defendant's position. Defendant contends that the towing permit scheme is only being applied to those drivers and firms that conduct substantial or consequential business in San Francisco. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the towing permit scheme is being applied to drivers and firms that perform minimal or transitory work in the City. Plaintiff also has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the permit fees are impermissible taxes, i.e. the fees are applied for general revenue purposes or are unreasonably excessive when compared to the regulating activity. 1 ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =301/HK

Defendant contends that the towing permit scheme is only being applied to those drivers and firms that conduct substantial or consequential business in San Francisco. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the towing permit scheme is being applied to drivers and firms that perform minimal or transitory work in the City.
